suppress.notes: JSON View plugins are versioned independently. They are not Grails Framework 3.2.3.
Async/Events plugins are not Grails 5.0.2 Framework.
The converters plugin version 5.0.0 is misidentified as the Grails 5.0.0 framework.
Grails Profiles use separate versioning. Project is actually Grails 6.2.3.
Custom FOLIO plugin versioned 1.0.0, incorrectly flagged as Grails 1.0.0.
Suppressing the incorrect spring-security framework CPE mapping for standalone subpackages. This ensures we are still alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the subpackage itself.
Suppressing the incorrect Spring Boot CPE mapping for starter packages. This ensures we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
Suppressing the incorrect Spring Framework CPE mapping for subpackages. This ensures we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
Suppressing the incorrect Netty framework CPE mapping for individual subpackages.
Suppressing the incorrect Undertow framework CPE mapping for subpackages.
Suppressing the incorrect Kafka CPE mapping for subpackages.
Grails 6 uses Spring 5.3.x. This RMI vulnerability is a known ghost hit for Spring 5. We suppress this specifically because we are not using HTTP Invoker.
Grails uses Spring under the hood, but Grails itself is not vulnerable to CVE-2016-1000027 as it does not expose or utilize the vulnerable Spring HTTP Invoker RMI endpoint.
Global suppression for CVE-2026-22740. Grails 6 runs on Spring MVC / standard Servlets; it does not utilize Spring WebFlux for handling requests or multipart file uploads.
Global suppression for CVE-2024-38816. The vulnerable Spring Framework behavior is mitigated/safeguarded indirectly by the Grails security plugin infrastructure using Spring's HTTP firewall.

Suppressing CVE-2025-41249, CVE-2026-22747 and CVE-2026-22732. These Spring Security vulnerabilities concern explicit endpoint authorization bypasses via raw Spring routing configurations. this application delegates inbound request validation and token checks up-stack to the FOLIO gateway layer, rendering local routing bypasses non-viable.

Suppressing Spring Core/Framework foundational class CVEs (CVE-2025-41242, CVE-2026-22737). These represent framework-level internal mechanisms that cannot be isolated or exploited via the standard Grails MVC controllers or FOLIO module layout.
CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40975. We COULD become vulnerable if we decide to autogenerate random secrets with ${random.value}, but we should NEVER do this in production anyway.
CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40973 because we currently do NOT use persistent sessions. DO NOT SET `server.servlet.session.persistent=true` IN APPLICATION.YML!
CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40977 because we currently do NOT use ApplicationPidFileWriter. DO NOT make use of ApplicationPidFileWriter in code that can make it to production systems.
Suppressing CVE-2026-40974 because we do not use Cassandra NoSQL database.
CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-22733 because we do not use CloudFoundry actuator endpoints.
CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-0603 because we do not use InlineIdsOrClauseBuilder.
